sections.paragraph:
ἀποκυλίω fut. ἀποκυλίσω; 1 aor. 3 sg. ἀπεκύλισε LXX; pf. pass. ἀποκεκύλισμαι (s. B-D-F §101 p. 46) (s. κυλίω; Diod S 14, 116, 6; Ps.-Apollod. 3, 15, 7; Lucian, Rhet. Praec. 3; Jos., Ant. 4, 284; 5, 359; LXX) roll away τὶ (Ἀρχαιολογικὴ Ἐφημερίς 1923, 39 λίθους [IV b.c.]) ἀ. τὸν λίθον (Gen 29:3, 8, 10) Mt 28:2; GPt 12:53; ἀ. τ. λίθον ἐκ τ. θύρας roll the stone away from the entrance Mk 16:3; cp. vs. 4. λίθος ἀποκεκυλισμένος ἀπὸ τ. μνημείου Lk 24:2 (so also the ptc. Mk 16:4 v.l.; the passive also has the same mng. as the intr. roll away: Diod S 20, 14, 6). S. ἀνακυλίω.—DELG s.v. κυλίνδω.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Ps.-Apollod Ps.-Apollod , myth., I/II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

ptc. ptc. = participle

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

intr. intr. = intransitive

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 114
